evals.name: detect-code-smells
evals.expected_output: Should identify multiple code smells: Long Method, Switch Statements, Feature Envy, Primitive Obsession, and suggest appropriate refactorings

assertions.name: identifies_long_method
assertions.description: Should identify the method is too long and doing too many things

assertions.name: identifies_switch_statements
assertions.description: Should identify switch/if-else on customer type as a smell

assertions.name: suggests_refactorings
assertions.description: Should suggest specific refactorings like Extract Method, Replace Conditional with Polymorphism

evals.name: recommend-design-pattern
evals.prompt: I have a class that needs to create different types of report objects (PDFReport, ExcelReport, HTMLReport) based on user configuration. The creation logic is getting complex with many conditionals. What design pattern should I use and how should I implement it?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend Factory Method or Abstract Factory pattern with clear explanation and implementation example

assertions.name: recommends_factory_pattern
assertions.description: Should recommend Factory Method or Abstract Factory pattern

assertions.name: provides_implementation
assertions.description: Should provide code example showing the pattern implementation

assertions.name: explains_benefits
assertions.description: Should explain why this pattern fits the problem

evals.name: refactoring-guidance
evals.prompt: I found this code smell in my codebase - a class called 'UserManager' that handles user authentication, user profile management, user notifications, and user reporting. It's over 500 lines. How should I refactor this?
evals.expected_output: Should identify this as Large Class and Divergent Change smells, recommend Extract Class refactoring, and provide step-by-step guidance

assertions.name: identifies_smells
assertions.description: Should correctly identify Large Class and Divergent Change

evals.name: solid-principle-violation
evals.expected_output: Should identify OCP violation (switch on type), DIP violation (direct instantiation), and suggest improvements

assertions.name: identifies_ocp_violation
assertions.description: Should identify Open/Closed Principle violation in generateReport method

assertions.name: identifies_dip_violation
assertions.description: Should identify Dependency Inversion Principle violation in constructor

assertions.name: suggests_improvements
assertions.description: Should suggest Strategy pattern and dependency injection

evals.name: pattern-selection
evals.prompt: I need to implement a feature where multiple objects need to be notified when a user's status changes. What design pattern should I use?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend Observer pattern with explanation and implementation example

assertions.name: explains_pattern
assertions.description: Should explain how Observer pattern works
